P2_Mexican_Mint.details: A small plant consistent with a young Mexican Mint is present in the black pot from T-4 onwards. A sensor is associated with this pot from T-4 onwards, aligning with the registry.

Unmonitored_Money_Plant.details: A Money Plant cutting is clearly visible in water propagation in the white cup from T-3 onwards, aligning with the registry.

systemic_losses.details: This pot, present in the EARLIEST image with a sensor and soil, is no longer visible in subsequent images. It represents a systemic loss from the observable biome.

new_introductions_interventions.details: Small white fragments, identified as eggshells, are consistently present on the soil surface of the black pot from EARLIEST through CURRENT images. This is an uncatalogued intervention.
The sensor, initially observed in the yellow/tan pot in the EARLIEST image, has been relocated to the black pot, positioned near the newly emerged plant from T-4 onwards.
A small plant, identified as P2: Mexican Mint, appears in the black pot from T-4 onwards, where previously only soil and eggshells were visible (excluding the yellow/tan pot).
A Money Plant cutting is introduced into the white cup for water propagation from T-3 onwards, where previously no plant was clearly visible.

observations_by_image.EARLIEST: Not visible, only soil and eggshells present in the black pot. A sensor is in a separate yellow/tan pot.
observations_by_image.T-5: Image is black, no visual data.
observations_by_image.T-4: A small plant with 4-5 roundish, dark green leaves has emerged. The sensor is now positioned near this plant in the black pot. Leaves appear turgid.
observations_by_image.T-3: Plant appears slightly larger than T-4, leaves remain dark green and turgid. No signs of stress.
observations_by_image.T-2: Continued subtle growth, leaves are healthy and well-hydrated. Overall posture is upright. Brighter lighting reveals more detail.
observations_by_image.T-1: Stable, no significant change in size or health from T-2. Leaves remain dark green and turgid. Lighting is darker.
observations_by_image.CURRENT: Stable, consistent with T-1. No visible signs of decline or significant new growth.

observations_by_image.EARLIEST: White cup partially visible, no plant clearly present.
observations_by_image.T-5: Image is black, no visual data.
observations_by_image.T-4: White cup not clearly visible, no plant present.
observations_by_image.T-3: A single Money Plant cutting with one prominent, healthy green leaf and a visible stem is present in water within the white cup.
observations_by_image.T-2: Stable, the leaf remains green and turgid. Water level appears consistent. Brighter lighting.
observations_by_image.T-1: Stable, no significant change from T-2. Leaf remains healthy. Lighting is darker.
observations_by_image.CURRENT: Stable, consistent with T-1. Leaf remains green and turgid, stem appears healthy in water.

biome_observations.black_pot_surface: Consistently covered with small white fragments (eggshells). Soil appears moist throughout the sequence where visible, with no signs of severe cracking or dryness. No incidental growth (weeds, moss, fungi) observed.
biome_observations.white_cup_contents: Contains clear water for propagation. No visible algae, cloudiness, or other incidental growth.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Wires and a white pen are visible in some frames. No significant debris, spills, or fungal presence on the immediate desk surface.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-5: Complete loss of visual data (black image).
temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-4: Introduction of the Mexican Mint plant into the black pot and relocation of the sensor to this pot. The yellow/tan pot is no longer visible.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of the Money Plant cutting into the white cup. Mexican Mint shows subtle growth.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Mexican Mint shows continued subtle growth, and lighting conditions improve significantly, enhancing visibility.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Both plants remain stable. Lighting conditions become darker again.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Both plants remain stable, no discernible changes in health or size.

visual_health_inference.P2_Mexican_Mint: The Mexican Mint plant exhibits good health from its first appearance (T-4) through the current image. Leaves are consistently dark green, turgid, and show no signs of wilting, yellowing, or necrosis. Subtle growth is observed between T-4 and T-2, indicating active metabolic processes. The soil appears adequately moist, supporting healthy root function.
visual_health_inference.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: The Money Plant cutting appears healthy and stable since its introduction (T-3). The single prominent leaf is green and turgid, with no visible discoloration or signs of rot on the submerged stem. The water in the cup remains clear, suggesting a clean propagation environment.

anomalies.description: Image [T-5] is entirely black, indicating a complete failure of image capture or data transmission for that specific timestamp.
The yellow/tan pot, present in the EARLIEST image and containing a sensor, is absent from all subsequent images. This represents an uncatalogued removal of an item from the biome.
The sensor's position changes from the yellow/tan pot (EARLIEST) to the black pot (T-4 onwards), indicating a manual intervention not explicitly documented in the registry.
The consistent presence of eggshells on the soil surface of the black pot is an uncatalogued material intervention.

vision_report.narrative_description: This chronological audit reveals a dynamic indoor botanical setup. The initial state ([EARLIEST]) shows a black pot with eggshells, a yellow/tan pot with a sensor, and a partially visible white cup. A significant data gap occurred at [T-5]. By [T-4], a young Mexican Mint plant has emerged in the black pot, and the sensor has been relocated to this pot. Subsequently, at [T-3], a Money Plant cutting was introduced into the white cup for water propagation. Over the observed period, both the Mexican Mint and Money Plant have maintained good health, exhibiting turgid, green foliage and subtle growth in the case of the Mexican Mint. The soil in the black pot appears consistently moist, and the propagation water in the white cup remains clear. Notable interventions include the consistent presence of eggshells in the black pot, the disappearance of the yellow/tan pot, and the relocation of the sensor. The overall biome appears stable and well-maintained, with both registered plants thriving in their respective environments.
